question_structure.context: As shown in Figure A, the mass of the laptop is $m$. Its cooling stand is generally equipped with four notches to adjust the angle. A student placed the laptop on the cooling stand and adjusted it to notch 4 (as indicated by the solid line in Figure B). When the angle between the inclined surface of the cooling stand and the horizontal direction is $\alpha$, the laptop remains stationary on the inclined surface of the cooling stand. The gravitational acceleration is $g$.
question_structure.sub_question_1: The magnitude of the elastic force and the frictional force acting on the computer at this moment.
question_structure.sub_question_2: When adjusted to position 1 (as shown by the dashed line in Figure B), the inclination angle of the heat dissipation base increases to θ (θ > α). At this point, a gentle push causes the laptop to slide down the inclined surface of the heat dissipation base at a constant speed. What is the coefficient of kinetic friction between the laptop and the inclined surface of the heat dissipation base?

sub_question_1.step_1: The computer on the cooling stand is in a state of equilibrium under the action of gravity $mg$, the supporting force $N$ from the cooling stand, and the frictional force $f$. Perform a force analysis on the computer hardware. According to the conditions of equilibrium, the magnitude of the elastic force acting on the computer is: $N = mg \cos \alpha$
sub_question_1.step_2: The magnitude of the frictional force is: $f = mg \sin \alpha$

sub_question_2.step_3: When card position 4 is adjusted to card position 1, the inclination angle of the heat dissipation base increases to $\theta$, at which point the computer slides down the inclined surface of the heat dissipation base at a constant speed. According to the equilibrium condition, we have: $mg \sin \theta = \mu mg \cos \theta$. The solution is: $\mu = \tan \theta$

image_captions: The image shows two panels, A and B. Panel A displays a 3D perspective view of a rectangular, blue laptop cooling pad with a black frame and a mesh top. The cooling pad has adjustable stands on the bottom, numbered 1 through 4. Panel B is a 2D diagram of the side view of the laptop cooling pad, showing it at different angles, with the angle labeled α. The diagram shows how the adjustable stands 1 and 4 alter the inclination of the pad.

result_quantity.name: Magnitude of the supporting force on the laptop
Magnitude of the frictional force on the laptop
Coefficient of kinetic friction between the laptop and the inclined surface
